manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Hobart's doorstep mountain for summit snow play, lookouts and quick winter checks from the city. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Use webcams and road updates because Pinnacle Road can close.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Accessible Mount Field alpine lake area for southern Tasmania snow play and winter walking. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Chains may be required and Lake Dobson Road can close.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Small southern Tasmanian ski field above Lake Dobson with downhill, cross-country and snow-play appeal. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Natural snow and volunteer-run operations make condition checks essential.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Tasmania's most resort-like snow destination with skiing, snowboarding, tobogganing and alpine-village facilities. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Carry chains in winter and check shuttle or road conditions before climbing Jacobs Ladder.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Iconic steep winter road ascent to the Ben Lomond plateau and its snowfields. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Chains are required in winter and walking the road is not permitted for safety.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Tasmanian alpine landscape with winter snowdrifts, mountain views and serious cold-weather walking conditions. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Best for prepared scenery and walking, not resort skiing.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Classic Cradle Mountain lake walk and photography stop when winter weather brings snow to the peaks. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Use shuttle, park-pass and weather guidance before heading in.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Southern wilderness snow destination with alpine plateaus, tarns and exposed winter walking. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Less structured than a resort and more weather exposed than city-side snow spots.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Broad Tasmanian highlands snow country with classic cold-climate winter scenery. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Remote, unserviced and best approached with conservative weather planning.

manual_intro: Hobart has both city-side snow checks and deeper Tasmanian alpine trips. This guide mixes close snow-play mountains, southern ski-field options and high-country parks where weather can change quickly.
place_description: Alpine-lake winter stop with snow atmosphere and access into the southern end of Cradle Mountain-Lake St Clair. Best time: mid-June to late September, depending on weather. Roads and tracks may be affected by snow or ice in winter.
